zh.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton於大約1300年出生在英國英格蘭肯特布羅姆利。他於1317年之前與Juliana Asshewy在英國素福爾可亞可利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1345年在英國英格蘭倫敦被列為mayor of london。他去世於大約1376年在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton于大约1300年出生在英国英格兰肯特布羅姆利。他于1317年之前与Juliana Asshewy在英国素福爾可亞可利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1345年在英国英格兰倫敦被列为mayor of london。他去世于大约1376年在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Lacer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of cord and string, derivedfrom Middle English lace ‘cord’ (Old French laz,las).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton was born about 1300, in Bromley, Kent, England, United Kingdom. He married Juliana Asshewy before 1317, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. In 1345, at the age of 46, his occupation is listed as mayor of london in London, England, United Kingdom. He died about 1376, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton est né environ 1300, à Bromley, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Juliana Asshewy avant 1317, à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. En 1345, à l’âge de 46 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mayor of london à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé environ 1376, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton wurde um 1300 in Bromley, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat vor 1317 in Yaxley, Suffolk, England Juliana Asshewy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1345, als er 46 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mayor of london in London, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb um 1376 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton è nato il circa 1300 nel Bromley, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Juliana Asshewy prima del 1317, nel Yaxley, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1345, all'età di 46 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mayor of london nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì circa 1376, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hamptonは約1300年にイギリスイギリスケントブロムリーで生まれた。1317年以前にイギリススフォルクヤクスリーでJuliana Asshewyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1345年，46歳の時，イギリスイギリスロンドンでの職業はmayor of londonと記録されている。彼は約1376年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton(은)는 대략 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 브롬리에서 태어났다. 그는 1317년 이전 영국 수폴크 야스리에서 Juliana Asshewy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1345년, 46세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 mayor of london이었다. 그는 대략 1376년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Bromley, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Juliana Asshewy antes 1317, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1345, com 46 anos, sua profissão aparece como mayor of london em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1376, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Le Lacer Baron Lane of Hampton родился около 1300 г. в местности бромли, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana Asshewy перед 1317 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1345 году, в возрасте 46 лет,  он работал по профессии mayor of london в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер около 1376 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 78 лет.

Se casó con Juliana Asshewy antes de 1317, en Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1345, a la edad de 46 años, su ocupación registrada era mayor of london en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió aproximadamente 1376, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 78 años.
